steps.title: Read the contrast
steps.content: Critics treat fine art (timeless museum works) and fashion (mass-produced, periodic styles) as separate worlds. Okuma is presented as a counterexample because her work appears in BOTH the Metropolitan Museum (fine art) and her online boutique (fashion/commerce).

steps.title: Pin down what verb the critics would use
steps.content: If Okuma's dual presence refutes the critics, the critics must be claiming the two domains rarely meet or overlap. We need a verb meaning "come together / cross paths."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — prevail: means to triumph or be widespread; it doesn't describe a relationship between two domains. ✗
  • Choice B — succumb: means to yield or give in — needs an opponent and doesn't describe overlap. ✗
  • Choice C — diverge: means to move apart; saying they "rarely diverge" would mean they usually meet, which is the opposite of the critics' view. ✗
  • Choice D — intersect: means to overlap or cross; "rarely intersect" captures the critics' separation thesis, which Okuma's museum-and-boutique presence directly challenges. ✓
That is the correct answer.
